module_title: Stressors Affecting Families and Family Interventions
learning_objectives: Differentiate between functional and dysfunctional family dynamics.
Facilitate psychoeducational interventions to support families.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content: Families influence health outcomes and coping. Nurses work with whole families, not just individual patients.

sections.title: Family Systems Concepts
sections.content: The family operates as a system with roles, rules, and patterns. Understanding these concepts guides assessment.

sections.title: Functional vs Dysfunctional Dynamics
sections.content: Healthy families communicate openly and provide support. Dysfunctional patterns like enmeshment or triangulation create stress.

sections.title: Family Assessment Tools
sections.content: Genograms and ecomaps reveal relationships, resources, and potential problems.

sections.title: Communication and Conflict
sections.content: Teaching respectful communication and problem-solving helps families manage disagreements.

sections.title: Crisis and Trauma
sections.content: Addiction, violence, and sudden illness disrupt family functioning. Interventions focus on safety and connection.

sections.title: Chronic Illness and Caregiving
sections.content: Long-term conditions require role adjustments and community resources to prevent caregiver strain.

sections.title: End-of-Life and Grief
sections.content: During terminal illness, families need support with decision making, hospice referrals, and bereavement.

sections.title: Psychoeducation and Community Support
sections.content: Teaching families about mental illness, coping skills, and local programs fosters resilience.

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.content: By partnering with families, nurses promote healthier interactions and improve patient outcomes.
